skill.name: flowio
skill.description: Parse Flow Cytometry Standard (FCS) files v2.0–3.1 and extract events/metadata for preprocessing workflows (e.g., when you need NumPy arrays, channel info, or CSV/DataFrame export from cytometry files).
skill.summary: Parse Flow Cytometry Standard (FCS) files v2.0–3.1 and extract events/metadata for preprocessing workflows using NumPy, Pandas, or CSV export.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 144 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation and Python code examples (SKILL.md, references/api_reference.md). The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings (12 high) are false positives caused by FCS file parameter naming conventions (PnE, PnS, PnR) that the scanner confused with encryption patterns. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings (130 medium) are false positives caused by markdown triple-backtick code fences being misinterpreted as Ruby shell execution. The 'system reconnaissance' and 'hardcoded URL' findings are standard documentation print statements and source attribution links. The 'dynamic import' finding is a static Python import statement. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected. Previous audit (flowio_audit_result_v1.json) confirms Production Ready grade (86/100) with all veto gates passing.

content.user_title: Parse and analyze Flow Cytometry Standard files
content.value_statement: Researchers struggle to extract structured data from FCS cytometry files due to complex binary formats. This skill reads FCS v2.0 to 3.1 files and converts event data into NumPy arrays, Pandas DataFrames, or CSV files for analysis.
actual_capabilities: Parse FCS v2.0, 3.0, and 3.1 files to extract event data matrices
Access channel metadata including labels, ranges, and scatter/fluorescence/time indices
Export cytometry events as NumPy arrays or Pandas DataFrames for downstream analysis
Read FCS metadata only for memory-efficient file inspection without loading events
Handle problematic FCS files with offset discrepancies and multi-dataset content
Create new FCS files from processed event data arrays
limitations: Cannot modify event data in-place within a FlowData object; requires creating a new FCS file
Does not perform advanced cytometry analysis such as compensation, gating, or clustering
Limited to FCS file format; does not support other flow cytometry data formats
Preprocessing transformations are basic; custom transforms require manual implementation

use_cases.title: Read and export FCS cytometry data
use_cases.description: Load FCS files from flow cytometry experiments and export event data as CSV or Pandas DataFrames for statistical analysis.
use_cases.target_user: Biomedical researcher needing to analyze cytometry data

use_cases.title: Inspect FCS file metadata without loading events
use_cases.description: Quickly read TEXT segment metadata from large FCS files to check instrument settings, acquisition dates, and channel configurations without loading event data.
use_cases.target_user: Lab technician validating FCS file contents

use_cases.title: Build custom cytometry data pipelines
use_cases.description: Parse FCS files, apply custom transformations, and write modified FCS files as part of an automated preprocessing pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Read FCS file and print basic information
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flowio to read an FCS file and print its version, event count, channel count, and channel names.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have an FCS file and want to quickly check its basic properties.

prompt_templates.title: Export FCS events to CSV file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flowio to read an FCS file, convert the events to a Pandas DataFrame with channel labels as column names, and save the result as a CSV file.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to analyze flow cytometry data in a statistical tool that accepts CSV input.

prompt_templates.title: Handle FCS files with offset errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flowio to read an FCS file that may have offset discrepancies or parsing errors. Implement proper error handling with fallback options for ignore_offset_discrepancy and ignore_offset_error.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have FCS files with inconsistent byte offsets between HEADER and TEXT segments.

prompt_templates.title: Process multi-dataset FCS files with event filtering
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flowio to read all datasets from a multi-dataset FCS file, apply preprocessing to each, filter events by scatter channel threshold, and write each filtered dataset as a new FCS file.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have an FCS file containing multiple experiment datasets that need individual preprocessing and filtering.

output_examples.input: Read FCS file metadata for sample.fcs
output_examples.output: File: sample.fcs | Version: 3.1 | Events: 50,000 | Channels: 8 | Channel names: FSC-A, SSC-A, FL1-A, FL2-A, FL3-A, FL4-A, FL5-A, Time | Instrument: CytoFLEX | Acquisition date: 15-JAN-2025

output_examples.input: Export FCS events from experiment.fcs to CSV with preprocessing enabled.
output_examples.output: FCS file read successfully. Event data shape: (50000, 8). Preprocessing applied: gain scaling and log transform. Exported 50000 events across 8 channels to experiment_events.csv.

output_examples.input: Filter FCS events by scatter threshold and create a new FCS file.
output_examples.output: Read 25000 events from raw_data.fcs. Applied median threshold on FSC-A channel. Retained 12500 events above threshold. Created filtered_data.fcs with same metadata and channel configuration.

best_practices: Always use try-except blocks when reading FCS files to handle FCSParsingError and DataOffsetDiscrepancyError.
Use only_text=True when you only need metadata to avoid loading large event data into memory.
Always check for multi-dataset files using read_multiple_data_sets() when working with FCS sources of unknown origin.
anti_patterns: Do not ignore offset errors by default; only use ignore_offset_error=True after confirming offset issues in your files.
Do not assume all FCS files follow the same channel naming convention; always inspect pnn_labels before processing.
Do not modify the events attribute directly on FlowData; use create_fcs() to produce files with modified data.

faq.question: What versions of FCS files does flowio support?
Can flowio perform compensation or gating on cytometry data?
faq.answer: No, flowio only reads and writes FCS files. For compensation and gating, use the FlowKit library which builds on top of FlowIO.

faq.question: How do I handle FCS files with offset errors?
faq.answer: Use the ignore_offset_discrepancy=True or ignore_offset_error=True parameters when creating a FlowData instance. These options tell the parser to tolerate byte offset mismatches.

faq.question: Can I read only the metadata from an FCS file without loading all events?
faq.answer: Yes, use FlowData(path, only_text=True) to parse only the TEXT segment metadata without loading event data into memory.

faq.question: How do I export FCS data to CSV?
faq.answer: Read the file with FlowData, call as_array() to get a NumPy array, create a Pandas DataFrame with pnn_labels as column names, and use to_csv() to save the file.

faq.question: Can flowio handle files with multiple datasets?
faq.answer: Yes, use the read_multiple_data_sets() function to read all datasets from a multi-dataset FCS file. Each dataset is returned as a separate FlowData instance.
